parameters.name: id
parameters.label: ID
parameters.description: The sequence # of the event, starting from 0 and incrementing by 1 for each event.

parameters.name: index
parameters.label: Index
parameters.description: The channel index of the incoming that caused the event.

parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: While the input is greater than 0 this channel goes to 1, ie when the input channel goes "On".

parameters.name: input
parameters.label: Input
parameters.description: The value of the input channel when the input went on (at the birth of the event). It is often the note velocity value. If you pass the Midi In into the , and set the Midi In option to output the velo

parameters.name: time
parameters.label: Time
parameters.description: Time in seconds from the start of the event.

parameters.name: adsr
parameters.label: ADSR
parameters.description: The value according to the Attack, Decay, Sustain, Release. It uses the parameters on the ADSR page, regulating the speed and values, with extended parameters: Attack Time, Attack Level, Decay Time, S

parameters.name: state
parameters.label: State
parameters.description: This is good for playing back movies. You divide your movie into 4 parts that correspond to the (0=attack, 1=decay, 2=sustain, 3=release) phases. The state channel outputs fractional values, so you ca

parameters.name: resetcondition
parameters.label: Reset Condition
parameters.description: Determines the reset behavior of using the 2nd Input Reset Trigger. This parameter is only active if there is an input connected to the 's 2nd input.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: When set to 'On' it resets the clearing all events.

parameters.name: resetpulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: Immediately resets the and clears all events in the frame it was clicked.

parameters.name: callbacks
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: The path to a containing onCreate() and onDestroy() callbacks for each event.

parameters.name: attacktime
parameters.label: Attack Time
parameters.description: Affects and channel. Time to rise to max attack level.

parameters.name: attacktunit
parameters.label: Attack Time Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: attacklevel
parameters.label: Attack Level
parameters.description: Affects channel. Peak attack level.

parameters.name: decaytime
parameters.label: Decay Time
parameters.description: Affects channel and channel. Time after peak to sustain level.

parameters.name: decaytunit
parameters.label: Decay Time Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: sustaintime
parameters.label: Sustain Time
parameters.description: Affects adsr channel.

parameters.name: sustaintunit
parameters.label: Sustain Time Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: sustainmin
parameters.label: Sustain Min
parameters.description: Affects channel. Level at start of sustain time.

parameters.name: sustainmax
parameters.label: Sustain Max
parameters.description: Affects channel. Level at end of sustain time.

parameters.name: releasetime
parameters.label: Release Time
parameters.description: Affects and channels.

parameters.name: releasetunit
parameters.label: Release Time Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: releaselevel
parameters.label: Release Level
parameters.description: Affects channel. Level at end of life cycle.

parameters.name: speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: Affects the speed of the event, letting you stretch out or shorten the life of an event.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).
